categories.name: Get Oriented
categories.description: Intro classes, explainers, and starting points for anyone new to AI — including real-world examples of what AI can do.

categories.name: Stay Current
categories.description: Newsletters, blogs, and podcasts to keep up with AI policy, governance, safety, and industry developments.

categories.name: Specific Industry Resources
categories.description: Sector-specific deep dives — how AI is being applied across China, biotech, semiconductors, national security, robotics, materials science, cybersecurity, and more.

categories.name: Policy Guides
categories.description: Practitioner guides, frameworks, and reference documents for AI policy work — NIST AI RMF, the AI Incident Database, transparency hubs, and how-to guides for navigating AI policy.

categories.name: AI Safety, Ethics, & Governance
categories.description: AI safety research, ethics frameworks, and governance perspectives shaping how AI gets built and deployed.

categories.name: AI in Education
categories.description: Organizations and frameworks helping educators teach AI to students at all levels — K–12 and beyond.

categories.name: Build Skills & Credentials
categories.description: Longer-form courses, certifications, and structured learning paths — for when you want a credential, not just a primer.

categories.name: AI Tools We've Tested
categories.description: Honest reviews of AI tools from fellows who've actually used them in policy work. Our differentiator: hands-on evaluations from inside government.

categories.name: Events & Conferences
categories.description: Major AI policy conferences, summits, and recurring meetups worth attending.

categories.name: Builders and Researchers
categories.description: Technical resources for fellows who want to understand AI under the hood or build with it.

format.course: A structured class, often spanning weeks with multiple modules
format.newsletter: Periodic email or Substack publication, typically weekly or biweekly
format.podcast: Audio show released on a regular schedule, often interviews or analysis
format.blog: Personal or institutional website with ongoing posts
format.video: Video content (YouTube, recorded lectures, video series)
format.tool: Software or AI service you can use directly
format.conference: In-person or virtual event with talks, panels, and networking
format.use-case: Real-world example or case study showing AI in action
format.guide: Step-by-step instructional content or reference document
format.database: Structured collection of records you can search or browse
format.organization: Institution, nonprofit, or program offering ongoing resources
format.report: In-depth analysis document, often with data and recommendations

level.beginner: No prior knowledge required; explains concepts from scratch
level.intermediate: Assumes some baseline familiarity with AI concepts
level.advanced: Technical or specialized; assumes significant background
level.all-levels: Accessible to readers across experience levels

cost.freemium: Free tier available; paid tier offers more features or content
cost.varies: Cost depends on the specific item, plan, or use

topic.ai-policy: AI laws, regulations, and policy proposals
topic.ai-governance: Frameworks for how AI is developed, deployed, and overseen
topic.ai-safety: Reducing harms and risks from advanced AI systems
topic.ai-ethics: Moral and societal questions raised by AI
topic.china: AI development, policy, and politics in China
topic.national-security: AI's role in defense, intelligence, and national security
topic.biotech: AI applications in biology, drug discovery, and life sciences
topic.health: AI in healthcare, medicine, and public health
topic.materials-science: AI for discovering and developing new materials
topic.semiconductors: AI hardware: chips, fabrication, and supply chains
topic.robotics: AI-powered physical systems and embodied agents
topic.cybersecurity: AI's role in attacking and defending computer systems
topic.education: Teaching AI in K-12, higher ed, and beyond
topic.labor: AI's effects on jobs, workers, and employment
topic.diversity-equity: Equity, inclusion, and representation in AI
topic.industry-strategy: Business and competitive dynamics of the AI industry
topic.developer-tools: Tooling for engineers building with AI
topic.agentic-ai: AI systems that take autonomous actions
topic.transparency: Disclosures, audits, and openness in AI systems
topic.general: Broad AI topics not tied to a specific domain
topic.use-cases: Real-world examples and applications of AI
topic.responsible-ai: Approaches to building AI that benefits society

resources.title: AI for Everyone
resources.description: Introductory course taught by Coursera and Google Brain co-founder Andrew Ng. Andrew is generally considered bullish on AI technology and believes catastrophic risks are overblown, with applications rather than models the proper subject of regulation.
resources.author: Andrew Ng

resources.title: Generative AI for Everyone
resources.description: Similar to AI for Everyone but updated after ChatGPT and image generation models became common.
resources.author: Andrew Ng

resources.title: 3Blue1Brown Deep Learning Series
resources.description: Video explainers on foundational deep learning concepts — neural networks, gradient descent, backpropagation — and newer topics like transformers. Visually beautiful and conceptually rigorous.

resources.title: AI: A Guide for Thinking Humans
resources.description: Accessible commentary on notable AI developments for intellectually curious non-specialists.
resources.author: Melanie Mitchell

resources.title: Understanding AI
resources.description: Accessible explanations of how AI works and its economic and societal impact for informed general readers.
resources.author: Timothy B. Lee

resources.title: Supercharging a curious researcher
resources.description: Dean Ball, an AI policy researcher, uses Claude Code to automate invoice management, create educational simulations, analyze data, monitor an art market, and replicate papers.

resources.title: Taking care of everyday work tasks
resources.description: Lenny's Newsletter readers (PMs and developers) share how they use Claude Code to clean up disk space, find conflict avoidance in meeting transcripts, organize notes, write job descriptions, analyze competitor ads, and build presentations.

resources.title: Job hunting with AI
resources.description: Claire Vo (Product Manager) recommends Claude Code for job hunting: writing coaching notes from interview transcripts, personalizing outreach, tracking application stats, building a personal site, and running mock interviews with voice mode.

resources.title: Keeping a tomato plant alive with AI
resources.description: Martin DeVido uses Claude Code running every 15 minutes to check soil moisture, temperature, and light, then adjust LEDs, irrigation, and a heating pad to keep a tomato plant healthy.

resources.title: Making music theory interactive
resources.description: Matthew Meadows, a developer and musician, built an Interactive Circle of Fifths for musicians, students, and composers in three days using AI assistance.

resources.title: Building a website to answer your wife's daily question
resources.description: Ryan Wigley built a website that tells his wife the current weather in Seattle and whether the mountains are visible.

resources.title: Making your own MRI data viewer
resources.description: Tobi Lutke (Shopify CEO) was frustrated that his annual MRI data needed a Windows-only program. He used Claude Code to build an HTML viewer that worked on Mac.

resources.title: Saving money on ThredUp
resources.description: Abigail Haddad built a Chrome extension that checks if a promotional price is available for clothes on ThredUp and redirects to it.

resources.title: Creating an iMessage Wrapped
resources.description: Jasmine Sun used Claude Code to write a program that generates a Spotify Wrapped-style analysis of iMessage history — top contacts, messaging patterns, vocabulary evolution. Processed locally on Mac.

resources.title: Driving financial modeling in Excel with AI
resources.description: Learning Journey AI's guide to using Claude Code for financial analysis in Excel.

resources.title: Don't Worry About the Vase
resources.description: Zvi's blog. Frequent roundups of AI-related news from Twitter and commentary on hot topics. Voluminous and opinionated.
resources.author: Zvi Mowshowitz

resources.title: Transformer
resources.description: News publication about the power and politics of transformative AI, with funding from Open Philanthropy (a prominent AI Safety nonprofit funder).

resources.title: Dario Amodei's site
resources.description: The CEO of Anthropic shares his thoughts on where AI is going. Long-form, ambitious essays from one of the most influential figures in frontier AI.
resources.author: Dario Amodei

resources.title: Hyperdimensional
resources.description: Dean Ball — author of the Trump Administration's AI Action Plan and a notable conservative expert on AI technology.
resources.author: Dean Ball

resources.title: AI as Normal Technology
resources.description: Grounded analysis treating AI as transformative-but-conventional tech, for readers wary of both hype and doom.
resources.author: Sayash Kapoor

resources.title: AI Policy Perspectives
resources.description: Personal-capacity essays from a range of thinkers on AI policy and governance.

resources.title: Extreme Friday AI
resources.description: Friday-shipped newsletter at the intersection of AI development and regulation.
resources.author: Anna Haensch

resources.title: Forecasting Research Institute
resources.description: Applies the science of forecasting to high-stakes AI decisions, for readers who like rigorous prediction methodology.

resources.title: Miles's Substack
resources.description: Former OpenAI policy researcher's commentary on AI governance, safety, and frontier policy.
resources.author: Miles Brundage

resources.title: Morgan's Substack
resources.description: AI policy analyst (IAPS) covering AI governance and policy issues.
resources.author: Morgan Livingston

resources.title: The Power Law
resources.description: Forecaster's analysis of AI, national security, and emerging-technology trends.
resources.author: Peter Wildeford

resources.title: Rising Tide
resources.description: Intermittent thoughts on navigating the transition to advanced AI from a former OpenAI board member.
resources.author: Helen Toner

resources.title: Threading the Needle
resources.description: Weekly analysis of the political economy of AI for policymakers and governance professionals.
resources.author: Anton Leicht

resources.title: The Algorithmic Bridge
resources.description: Accessible, human-centered analysis of AI advancements for enthusiasts and professionals seeking depth without jargon.
resources.author: Alberto Romero

resources.title: Mixture of Experts
resources.description: Interdisciplinary takes on AI/computing trends and their impacts on science, policy, and society.
resources.author: MIT FutureTech

resources.title: Second Thoughts
resources.description: Accessible, jargon-free analysis of major AI issues for general readers.
resources.author: Steve Newman

resources.title: Steve Blank
resources.description: Customer-development methodology and 'secret history of Silicon Valley' essays for entrepreneurs and innovation practitioners.
resources.author: Steve Blank

resources.title: Theory of the Game
resources.description: Frameworks for entrepreneurship, investing, and life across tech, AI, politics, and philosophy.
resources.author: Reid Hoffman

resources.title: Very Sane AI Newsletter
resources.description: Conceptual essays on AI ideas focused on the 'what and why' rather than implementation.
resources.author: SE Gyges

resources.title: Weighty Thoughts
resources.description: VC perspective on AI, semiconductors, and deep tech for engineers, fund managers, and policymakers.
resources.author: James Wang

resources.title: AI Newsletter
resources.description: Weekly digest of top AI papers, tools, news, and agent developments for researchers and practitioners.
resources.author: Elvis Saravia

resources.title: Lenny's Newsletter
resources.description: Deeply researched product, growth, and career advice for product managers, founders, and ambitious builders.
resources.author: Lenny Rachitsky

resources.title: The Product Compass
resources.description: Actionable playbooks on product management and AI product-building for PMs at all career stages.
resources.author: Paweł Huryn

resources.title: Simon Willison's Newsletter
resources.description: Practitioner coverage of LLMs, Python, and open-source tooling for engineers staying current with applied AI.
resources.author: Simon Willison

resources.title: Artificial Bureaucracy
resources.description: History of computing, for readers interested in tech's historical context and evolution.
resources.author: Kevin Baker

resources.title: Blood in the Machine
resources.description: Reporting on AI, tech, labor, and power for 'everyone Silicon Valley is happening to.'
resources.author: Brian Merchant

resources.title: Notes on AI Readiness
resources.description: aiEDU CEO on preparing the education system and workforce for AI's human impacts.
resources.author: Alex Kotran

resources.title: Reboot
resources.description: Essays on the future of tech advancing 'responsible optimism' for readers wanting thoughtful cultural critique.

resources.title: Under Development
resources.description: Coverage of economic development and emerging technology for readers interested in growth, innovation, and global development.
resources.author: Deena Mousa

resources.title: AI Secret
resources.description: Newsletter covering practical AI tools and applications. (Subscribe via email — review/categorize further once Claire confirms scope.)

resources.title: ChinAI
resources.description: China-focused AI policy newsletter; weekly translations and analysis of Chinese AI developments.

resources.title: ChinaTalk
resources.description: Long-form podcast and newsletter on China policy, tech, and US-China dynamics.

resources.title: AI Safety in China
resources.description: Tracker of China's AI safety and governance developments for international policy and safety researchers.
resources.author: Concordia AI

resources.title: China AI Bulletin
resources.description: Coverage of AI development, governance, and safety in China to inform international AI governance discussion.

resources.title: Concurrent
resources.description: Essays on parallel tech and cultural currents shaping Silicon Valley, China, and AI ecosystems.
resources.author: afra

resources.title: Interconnected
resources.description: Analysis of tech, investing, geopolitics, and US-Asia relations for cross-Pacific watchers.
resources.author: Kevin Xu

resources.title: Matt Sheehan's Newsletter
resources.description: Carnegie analyst's takes on China, technology, and global AI policy.
resources.author: Matt Sheehan

resources.title: Schneier on Security
resources.description: Bruce Schneier's long-running blog — the canonical reference for security thinking, increasingly covering AI's security implications.
resources.author: Bruce Schneier

resources.title: Machine Speed
resources.description: Weekly threat-landscape update on AI-enabled cyberattacks, defenses, and lagging policy.
resources.author: daria

resources.title: ITU Cybersecurity Skills Development
resources.description: International Telecommunication Union resources on cybersecurity skills and capacity development.
resources.author: ITU

resources.title: In the Pipeline
resources.description: Long-running pharma/biotech blog on Science.org — covers drug discovery, including AI's growing role.

resources.title: BlueDot Detects COVID-19 Before the World Is Aware
resources.description: Case study of how BlueDot's AI surveillance system detected the COVID-19 outbreak early.

resources.title: DH Connect (Nivira Health)
resources.description: Digital health connectivity platform. (Description needs review/expansion by Claire.)

resources.title: Semianalysis
resources.description: Deep coverage of the semiconductor industry — capacity, fabs, supply chain, and AI hardware economics.

resources.title: Asianometry
resources.description: YouTube channel famous for semiconductor industry explainers; the author also chases many other interesting rabbit holes.

resources.title: Special Competitive Studies Project
resources.description: Eric Schmidt's post-NSCAI effort to convince the government to take transformative technologies seriously. Publishes thought papers and runs free events in DC.

resources.title: General Robots
resources.description: Benjie Holson's series on building a general-purpose robot company — for robotics founders, engineers, and hardware-startup readers.
resources.author: Benjie Holson

resources.title: It Can Think!
resources.description: Chris Paxton on robotics and embodied-AI progress for readers following automation.
resources.author: Chris Paxton

resources.title: Stratechery
resources.description: Ben Thompson has been analyzing the business and strategy sides of tech and media for over a decade. His conceptual frameworks and interviews are great ways to orient yourself.
resources.author: Ben Thompson

resources.title: Dwarkesh Podcast
resources.description: The frontier AI industry's favorite technical podcast.
resources.author: Dwarkesh Patel

resources.title: Jasmi.news
resources.description: Jasmine Sun's unique anthropological lens on the tech sector.
resources.author: Jasmine Sun

resources.title: One Useful Thing
resources.description: Wharton professor Ethan Mollick's accessible takes on AI in business and education.
resources.author: Ethan Mollick

resources.title: TrailGuard AI
resources.description: Camera-trap AI system for detecting poachers in protected areas.

resources.title: PAWS — Predicting Poaching Hotspots
resources.description: Harvard SEAS research on using AI to predict poaching hotspots and prevent wildlife crime.

resources.title: Accelerating Materials Discovery with Microsoft
resources.description: Podcast on Microsoft's machine learning–assisted material discovery, particularly lithium-ion battery electrolytes. Host Taylor Sparks is a good follow.

resources.title: The Future of Material Scientists in an Age of AI
resources.description: Short perspective on what AI could do for materials science. Generally correct, though some hypothesis-generation tooling may come before the robotics advances it predicts.

resources.title: Diffuse AI (formerly ML4Sci)
resources.description: Charles Yang covers ML applied to science. A hidden gem; one of the top metascientists out there.
resources.author: Charles Yang

resources.title: Shalini's Substack
resources.description: Shalini Sharma on AI applications in materials science.
resources.author: Shalini Sharma

resources.title: AI Safety Newsletter
resources.description: News roundup on AI safety developments for researchers, policymakers, and safety-focused industry readers.
resources.author: Center for AI Safety

resources.title: ML Safety Newsletter
resources.description: Roundup of machine-learning safety research developments for ML researchers and safety-focused engineers.
resources.author: Dan Hendrycks

resources.title: Cosmos Institute
resources.description: Philosophy-meets-AI publication from the 'Academy for Philosopher-Builders,' focused on AI for human flourishing.

resources.title: Secure Trajectories by Sondera
resources.description: Research on the systems and mechanics of agent control, for safety researchers working on autonomous systems.
resources.author: Josh Devon

resources.title: TeachAI
resources.description: Coalition working on AI literacy in education. Steering committee includes Code.org, Khan Academy, ETS, and ISTE.

resources.title: AI for Education
resources.description: For-profit consultancy helping educators adopt AI. Many educator partnerships and a helpful blog.

resources.title: MIT RAISE Initiative
resources.description: Many helpful publications, resources, and events. Runs a global AI hackathon.

resources.title: AI4K12
resources.description: 'Five big ideas in AI' framework commonly used by K-12 educators to categorize AI concepts.

resources.title: Data Center Watch
resources.description: Tracks data center development, including the AI infrastructure buildout.

resources.title: AI Incident Database
resources.description: Catalogs harms or near-harms realized in the real world by AI systems. Essential reference for AI policy and safety work.

resources.title: Berkman Klein Transparency Hub
resources.description: Resources on AI transparency from Harvard's Berkman Klein Center for Internet & Society.

resources.title: Berkman Klein Center for Internet & Society
resources.description: Harvard's premier research center on the internet, AI, and society. Publications, fellowships, and projects on responsible tech.

resources.title: NIST
resources.description: National Institute of Standards and Technology — the home of the NIST AI Risk Management Framework and core US AI standards work.

resources.title: Kapor Foundation — Responsible AI
resources.description: Resources and reports on responsible AI development with an equity lens.

resources.title: Tech Diplomacy
resources.description: Resources on technology diplomacy and the international governance of emerging tech.

resources.title: Global AI Index (Tortoise Media)
resources.description: Cross-country comparative index ranking nations on AI investment, innovation, and implementation.

resources.title: The AI Table
resources.description: Community/resource focused on diversity in AI. (Description needs review.)

resources.title: Four books on AI, bias, and ethics by women authors
resources.description: Curated reading list of foundational books on AI ethics and bias by women authors.
resources.author: Anisa Abeytia (curator)

resources.title: IAPP AIGP & Dr. David Prep Course
resources.description: Prep course for the IAPP Artificial Intelligence Governance Professional (AIGP) certification.

resources.title: AI Safety: Intro to Transformative AI
resources.description: BlueDot Impact's intro course on transformative AI and potential catastrophic risks. Their AI Safety mindset largely dominated the Biden administration's approach to frontier AI.
resources.author: BlueDot Impact

resources.title: BlueDot Impact
resources.description: Educational organization focused on transformative AI. Multiple courses on AI governance, economic impacts of AI, and AI alignment.

resources.title: CAIDP — AI Policy Clinic & Index
resources.description: Center for AI and Digital Policy. Runs an AI policy clinic and publishes the AI and Democratic Values Index.

resources.title: DC AI Guide
resources.description: Guide to the AI policy ecosystem in Washington, DC. (Description needs review.)

resources.title: AI Productivity Secrets
resources.description: Resource on practical AI productivity workflows. (Description needs review.)

resources.title: Manus AI
resources.description: TODO — fellow review needed. Notes from Claire's testing: [add review].

resources.title: Open Evidence
resources.description: TODO — fellow review needed. Medical AI search/evidence tool. Notes from testing: [add review].

resources.title: Gamma
resources.description: TODO — fellow review needed. AI-powered presentation/document generator. Notes from testing: [add review].

resources.title: Owl.ai
resources.description: TODO — fellow review needed. Research-focused AI tool. Notes from testing: [add review].

resources.title: Claude Code
resources.description: TODO — fellow review needed. Anthropic's agentic coding assistant. Many use cases under 'Get Oriented'. Notes from testing: [add review].

resources.title: IAPP Global Summit 2026
resources.description: Annual flagship summit of the International Association of Privacy Professionals. Major venue for AI governance and privacy policy.

resources.title: Data Community DC
resources.description: DC-area data and AI community running regular meetups, workshops, and events. Also active on Luma.

resources.title: EAAI Symposium on Educational Advances in AI
resources.description: One of the only symposiums focused on teaching and using AI in K–postgraduate education.

resources.title: Agentic AI Course
resources.description: Andrew Ng course where you build agentic AI systems that take action through iterative, multi-step workflows.
resources.author: Andrew Ng

resources.title: DeepLearning.AI
resources.description: Many helpful technical courses that dive deep into the weeds. Subscribing to the blog is highly recommended.

resources.title: Huyen Chip's Blog
resources.description: Huyen is an accomplished AI industry professional and author. Her blog is great for people building production AI applications.
resources.author: Huyen Chip

resources.title: Interconnects
resources.description: Nathan Lambert (Allen AI) covers all the latest trends in AI development from a researcher's lens without the hype.
resources.author: Nathan Lambert

resources.title: Ahead of AI
resources.description: Sebastian Raschka has been teaching machine learning with Python for over a decade. Strong technical depth.
resources.author: Sebastian Raschka

resources.title: Elite AI Assisted Coding
resources.description: Practical coverage of AI-assisted coding tools and workflows for developers and technical professionals.
resources.author: Eleanor Berger

resources.title: Import AI
resources.description: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark's weekly deep-dive into cutting-edge AI research and its policy implications.
resources.author: Jack Clark

resources.title: AI Evaluation Flash Cards
resources.description: Hamel Husain and Shreya Shankar distill core ideas from their AI Evals course into handy flashcards. Evaluating modern AI systems is tricky.
resources.author: Hamel Husain & Shreya Shankar

resources.title: AI Reading List for Policy Professionals
resources.description: Nicholas Wagner's curated GitHub repo of AI resources for policymakers and government professionals. The original source for many resources in this library.
resources.author: Nicholas Wagner
